title: What is REST?
description: Understand the principles of RESTful architecture.

slides.title: REST – Representational State Transfer 🌐
slides.content:
# RESTful APIs

REST is an architectural style for designing networked applications. It relies on stateless, client-server communication, typically over HTTP.

Key principles:
- **Resources** are identified by URLs (e.g., `/users`).
- **HTTP methods** define actions (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE).
- **Stateless** – each request contains all necessary information.
- **Representations** – resources can be represented in different formats (JSON, XML).

slides.title: HTTP Methods and Their Meanings 🛠️
slides.content:
## Common HTTP Methods in REST

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| GET    | Retrieve a resource | `GET /users` – list users, `GET /users/1` – get user 1 |
| POST   | Create a new resource | `POST /users` with data in body |
| PUT    | Replace an entire resource | `PUT /users/1` with full data |
| PATCH  | Partially update a resource | `PATCH /users/1` with only fields to change |
| DELETE | Remove a resource | `DELETE /users/1` |

Responses should use appropriate HTTP status codes.

quizData.question: Which HTTP method is used to create a new resource?
quizData.explanation: POST is typically used to create a new resource.
